chapters.title: Addition of  Numbers Total not Exceeding 9
Addition of Numbers, the Total not Exceeding 20
Number that come Before, Between and After

topics.name: Pre-Mathematical Concepts
topics.summary: Introduction to concepts before formal mathematical operations, building upon existing knowledge.

topics.name: Comparing 2D and 3D Shapes
topics.summary: Pupils can compare two dimensional and three dimensional shapes. (It is not compulsory to express with Mathematical terminals)

topics.name: Spatial Reasoning
topics.summary: Identifying objects under a table and determining groups with fewer items.

topics.name: Drawing and Size Comparison
topics.summary: Drawing objects based on size comparisons (smaller, bigger, thinner).

topics.name: Quantity Comparison
topics.summary: Drawing a lesser or greater number of items compared to a given set.

topics.name: Shape Recognition
topics.summary: Identifying and matching objects with similar shapes (ball, brick).

topics.name: Shape Matching
topics.summary: Matching objects with similar 3D geometric shapes and identifying same geometric shapes.

topics.name: Visual Discrimination
topics.summary: Matching pictures that look alike and identifying different shapes in a series.

topics.name: Color and Shape Association
topics.summary: Identifying colors of shapes and coloring similar shapes in a picture.

topics.name: Pattern Recognition
topics.summary: Observing and continuing a series of shapes.

topics.name: Introduction to Numbers 1 to 5
topics.summary: Matching pictures to understand the concept of counting.

topics.name: Counting and Number Recognition (1-5)
topics.summary: Counting objects and associating them with corresponding numbers (1 to 5).

topics.name: Matching Quantities
topics.summary: Matching sets of objects with the same quantity and matching numbers with corresponding quantities.

topics.name: Number Identification
topics.summary: Identifying and writing the number of objects in a picture.

topics.name: Counting objects up to 5
topics.summary: Counting objects in a picture and writing the corresponding number (1 to 5) in blank boxes. Matching objects with their corresponding numbers. Writing numbers 1 to 5 in blank boxes.

topics.name: Identifying different numbers of objects
topics.summary: Counting objects in each box and identifying the box with a different number of objects. Drawing the same shapes of next number in the blank boxes.

topics.name: Number Sequence
topics.summary: Counting toffees and writing their number. Writing numbers in blank boxes in an order/sequence.

topics.name: Counting objects in columns and rows
topics.summary: Counting pictures in each column and writing their number in the blank box. Writing correct numbers in the blank boxes based on a pattern.

topics.name: Numbers from 6 to 9
topics.summary: Counting objects in a picture and identifying the number of each category. Matching pictures with the same pictures or dots.

topics.name: Counting objects up to 9
topics.summary: Observing a picture, counting each thing, and writing the number in the blank box (up to 9).

topics.name: Counting to Seven
topics.summary: Counting objects and representing the quantity with the number 7.

topics.name: Counting to Eight
topics.summary: Counting objects and representing the quantity with the number 8.

topics.name: Counting to Nine
topics.summary: Counting objects and representing the quantity with the number 9.

topics.name: Matching Numbers and Quantities (6-9)
topics.summary: Matching the number of fingers shown with the corresponding numeral from 6 to 9.

topics.name: Writing Numbers (6-9)
topics.summary: Practicing writing the numerals from 6 to 9.

topics.name: Identifying Different Quantities
topics.summary: Counting objects in sets and identifying the set with a different quantity.

topics.name: Drawing Quantities
topics.summary: Drawing a specific number of objects based on a given quantity.

topics.name: Writing Numbers to Represent Quantities
topics.summary: Counting objects and writing the corresponding numeral.

topics.name: Writing Numbers 1 to 9
topics.summary: Practicing writing the numerals from 1 to 9 in ascending and descending order.

topics.name: Counting Objects and Identifying Numbers
topics.summary: Counting objects and matching them to corresponding numbers, identifying missing numbers in a sequence.

topics.name: Before, After, and Between
topics.summary: Understanding the concepts of 'before', 'after', and 'between' in the context of a sequence of numbers using a train bogie example.

topics.name: More, Less, and Equal
topics.summary: Comparing the number of objects to understand the concepts of 'more than', 'less than', and 'equal'.

topics.name: Addition of Numbers (Total not Exceeding 9)
topics.summary: Pupils can say that if 1 is added to a number, we get the next number. They can count each bead in a chain and add them. They can write two numbers (upto 9) horizontally or vertically and add them. They can say how much is to be added to a number to get desired number. They can add things in two different groups and their total. They can add different numbers to get the same total. They understand that zero added to any number gives the same number.

topics.name: Comparing Sets
topics.summary: Comparing two sets of objects to determine which has more, less, or equal items.

topics.name: Identifying More
topics.summary: Counting pictures and identifying the set with more items, marking it with '9'.

topics.name: Number Order
topics.summary: Counting boxes, writing numbers, and reading them in ascending and descending order.

topics.name: Next, Middle, and Previous Numbers
topics.summary: Identifying the next, middle, and previous numbers in a sequence.

topics.name: Identifying Bigger and Smaller Numbers
topics.summary: Identifying the bigger and smaller of two given numbers.

topics.name: Identifying Biggest and Least Numbers
topics.summary: Identifying the biggest of three numbers and the least of three numbers.

topics.name: Number Sequencing
topics.summary: Extending a sequence of numbers from smallest to biggest.

topics.name: Ordering Numbers (Smallest to Biggest)
topics.summary: Arranging numbers from smallest to biggest.

topics.name: Ordering Numbers (Biggest to Smallest)
topics.summary: Arranging numbers from biggest to smallest.

topics.name: Introduction to Zero
topics.summary: Understanding the concept of zero as 'none' or 'nothing'.

topics.name: Representing Zero
topics.summary: Counting beads and representing the absence of beads with the number zero.

topics.name: Counting Objects
topics.summary: Counting rats and balloons and writing their number in the blank box.

topics.name: Adding 1 to a Group of Beads
topics.summary: Adding 1 to a group of beads and counting the total number of beads.

topics.name: Adding Two Groups of Objects
topics.summary: Adding one group of things to another group and counting the total.

topics.name: Vertical and Horizontal Addition
topics.summary: Calculating the total of numbers by drawing dots and understanding vertical and horizontal addition.

topics.name: Adding Zero
topics.summary: Understanding that adding zero to any number results in the number itself.

topics.name: Matching Numbers and Objects
topics.summary: Matching numbers with corresponding quantities of objects.

topics.name: Addition of Two Numbers
topics.summary: Adding two single-digit numbers and writing the sum.

topics.name: Finding Missing Addends
topics.summary: Determining the missing number in an addition equation.

topics.name: Identifying Different Sums
topics.summary: Adding pairs of numbers and identifying the pair with a different sum.

topics.name: Subtraction of Numbers up to 9
topics.summary: Understanding and performing subtraction of numbers up to 9 using pictures and examples.

topics.name: Horizontal and Vertical Subtraction
topics.summary: Performing subtraction both horizontally and vertically.

topics.name: Subtraction Practice
topics.summary: Practicing subtraction problems.

topics.name: Matching Subtraction Problems with Answers
topics.summary: Matching subtraction problems with the same answer.

topics.name: Identifying Different Differences
topics.summary: Subtracting pairs of numbers and identifying the pair with a different difference.

topics.name: Filling in Missing Numbers in Subtraction
topics.summary: Filling in the missing number in a subtraction equation.

topics.name: Introduction to Numbers 10 to 20
topics.summary: Understanding the concept of numbers from 10 to 20 using bundles of ten and loose objects.

topics.name: Counting and Circling Groups of Ten
topics.summary: Identifying and circling groups of ten objects.

topics.name: Representing Numbers 10-14 with Bundles and Sticks
topics.summary: Representing numbers from 10 to 14 using bundles of ten sticks and individual sticks.

topics.name: Addition of Numbers (Total not Exceeding 20)
topics.summary: Adding two numbers with a sum not exceeding 20, using bundles of sticks and loose sticks.

topics.name: Subtraction of Numbers (Not Exceeding 20)
topics.summary: Subtracting a single-digit number from a two-digit number, using bundles of sticks and loose sticks.

topics.name: Counting to 20 with Bundles and Sticks
topics.summary: Counting sticks and bundles of sticks to represent numbers from 15 to 20. Understanding that adding 1 to 9 results in 10.

topics.name: Tens and Ones (11-12)
topics.summary: Understanding tens and ones using bundles and loose sticks to represent numbers 11 and 12. Reading and writing these numbers.

topics.name: Tens and Ones (13-16)
topics.summary: Understanding tens and ones using bundles and loose sticks to represent numbers 13, 14, 15 and 16. Reading and writing these numbers.

topics.name: Tens and Ones (17-20)
topics.summary: Understanding tens and ones using bundles and loose sticks to represent numbers 17, 18, 19 and 20. Recognizing 20 as two tens. Reading and writing these numbers.

topics.name: Identifying and Writing Numbers 10 to 20
topics.summary: Practice identifying and writing numbers from 10 to 20, including filling in missing numbers in a sequence.

topics.name: Number Sequencing
topics.summary: Identifying the first, last, and middle numbers in a given sequence.

topics.name: Comparing Numbers Less Than 20
topics.summary: Comparing two numbers less than 20 and identifying the bigger number.

topics.name: Comparing Numbers
topics.summary: Identifying bigger, smaller, biggest, and smallest numbers from a given set of numbers.

topics.name: Identifying Differences
topics.summary: Identifying the object or group that is different from others in a row.

topics.name: Addition of Numbers (up to 20)
topics.summary: Adding two numbers where the total does not exceed 20.

topics.name: Horizontal Addition
topics.summary: Adding two numbers horizontally.

topics.name: Adding Numbers Using Bundles and Loose Sticks
topics.summary: Adding two numbers using bundles of sticks (representing tens) and loose sticks (representing ones).

topics.name: Adding Two Numbers
topics.summary: Adding two numbers and writing the answer in a box.

topics.name: Adding Two Numbers and Coloring
topics.summary: Adding two numbers and coloring the part of a figure (turtle) that contains the answer.

topics.name: Adding Pairs of Numbers and Identifying Different Answers
topics.summary: Adding pairs of numbers in a row and identifying the different answer.

topics.name: Subtraction of Numbers not Exceeding 20
topics.summary: Subtracting a single-digit number from a two-digit number using bundles and loose sticks.

topics.name: Subtraction by Counting Remaining Objects
topics.summary: Subtracting numbers by looking at pictures and counting the remaining objects.

topics.name: Subtracting Numbers
topics.summary: Subtracting two numbers.

topics.name: Subtracting Pairs of Numbers and Identifying Different Answers
topics.summary: Subtracting pairs of numbers and identifying the different answer.

topics.name: Introduction of Tens from 10 to 100
topics.summary: Counting in tens and writing the numbers in boxes. Understanding the numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 and 100.

topics.name: Counting Tens and Identifying Different Boxes
topics.summary: Counting the tens in boxes and identifying the box which is different.

topics.name: Counting Grids
topics.summary: Counting the grids and writing the correct number.

topics.name: Counting Beads in Chains
topics.summary: Counting the beads in each chain and writing the correct number.

topics.name: Matching Tens with Correct Numbers
topics.summary: Matching each group of tens with the correct number.

topics.name: Writing Correct Numbers in Blank Spaces
topics.summary: Looking at the tens in each row and writing the correct number in the blank spaces.

topics.name: Numbers from 20 to 100
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks and loose sticks, writing the number, and saying how many tens and ones are there in each number.

topics.name: Identifying Times of a Day
topics.summary: Pupils can identify different times of a complete day (morning, afternoon, daytime, evening, night) and say what people do at those times.

topics.name: Daily Schedule
topics.summary: Pupils learn what they must do at what time.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Sticks (66-70)
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 66 to 70. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Sticks (71-75)
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 71 to 75. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Sticks (76-80)
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 76 to 80. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Sticks (81-85)
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 81 to 85. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Sticks (86-90)
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 86 to 90. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Sticks (91-95)
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 91 to 95. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Counting with Bundles and Loose Sticks
topics.summary: Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 96 to 100. Writing numbers as sums of tens and ones.

topics.name: Writing Missing Numbers in a Grid (1-100)
topics.summary: Identifying and writing missing numbers in a grid from 1 to 100 to understand the correct order of numbers.

topics.name: Identifying Correct Numbers from Pictures
topics.summary: Counting pictures and selecting the corresponding correct number from a given set of three numbers.

topics.name: Completing Number Series
topics.summary: Filling in the missing numbers in a given number series.

topics.name: Identifying Numbers Before, After, and Between
topics.summary: Identifying the numbers that come before, after, or between given numbers.

topics.name: Writing Numbers in Letters (1-20)
topics.summary: Writing the numbers from 1 to 20 in their corresponding word form.

topics.name: Numbers Before, Between, After
topics.summary: Identifying numbers before, between, and after given numbers in a real-world scenario (seats in rows).

topics.name: Counting and Comparing Numbers
topics.summary: Counting dots, writing numbers, and identifying bigger and smaller numbers.

topics.name: Previous, Next, and Middle Numbers
topics.summary: Identifying the number before, after, and in between given numbers.

topics.name: Identifying Smaller and Bigger Numbers
topics.summary: Drawing around the smaller number and ticking the bigger number from a pair of numbers.

topics.name: Identifying Smallest and Biggest Numbers
topics.summary: Identifying the smallest and biggest number from a set of five numbers.

topics.name: Ordering Numbers
topics.summary: Arranging numbers from smallest to biggest and biggest to smallest.

topics.name: Non-standard Measuring Tools
topics.summary: Understanding and using non-standard measuring tools like hand-breadth, handspan, cubit, and foot to measure the lengths of things.

topics.name: Comparing Weight
topics.summary: Identifying heavier objects by observation.

topics.name: Comparing Volume
topics.summary: Identifying vessels that hold more or less water/coffee.

topics.name: Measuring Distance with Cubit and Stick
topics.summary: Measuring leaped distances using a cubit and a stick and recording the observations.

topics.name: Estimating Length with Pencil
topics.summary: Guessing and measuring the length of objects using a pencil as a tool.

topics.name: Comparing Length to a Pencil
topics.summary: Identifying objects shorter than a pencil.

topics.name: Estimating Weight
topics.summary: Guessing which of two vegetables is lighter or heavier.

topics.name: Estimating Volume Filling Time
topics.summary: Guessing which vessel will fill a bucket faster and verifying by actual filling.

1. How many bogies does the train have?

2. What is the number of the bogie next to the engine?

3. What is the number of the last bogie?

4. What is number of the bogie between the bogies numbered 4 and 6 ?

5. What is the number of the bogie before the bogie numbered 5 ?

6. What are the numbers of the bogies between the first and the last bogie ?

7. What are the numbers of all the bogies that are bofore the bogie numbered 4?

8. What are the numbers on the last two bogies of the train?

9. What is the number of the bogie that is after the bogie numbered 2 ?
10.Between which bogies does the bogie numbered 6 lie?

action that takes place from first to the last (as 1, 2 and 3).

(e) Guess how long are a slate and a table, in terms of a pencil as a tool.

topics.concept_summary: Introduction to concepts before formal mathematical operations, building upon existing knowledge.
Pupils can compare two dimensional and three dimensional shapes. (It is not compulsory to express with Mathematical terminals)
Identifying objects under a table and determining groups with fewer items.
Drawing objects based on size comparisons (smaller, bigger, thinner).
Drawing a lesser or greater number of items compared to a given set.
Identifying and matching objects with similar shapes (ball, brick).
Matching objects with similar 3D geometric shapes and identifying same geometric shapes.
Matching pictures that look alike and identifying different shapes in a series.
Identifying colors of shapes and coloring similar shapes in a picture.
Observing and continuing a series of shapes.
Matching pictures to understand the concept of counting.
Counting objects and associating them with corresponding numbers (1 to 5).
Matching sets of objects with the same quantity and matching numbers with corresponding quantities.
Identifying and writing the number of objects in a picture.
Counting objects in a picture and writing the corresponding number (1 to 5) in blank boxes. Matching objects with their corresponding numbers. Writing numbers 1 to 5 in blank boxes.
Counting objects in each box and identifying the box with a different number of objects. Drawing the same shapes of next number in the blank boxes.
Counting toffees and writing their number. Writing numbers in blank boxes in an order/sequence.

topics.topic_name: Counting objects in columns and rows
topics.concept_summary: Counting pictures in each column and writing their number in the blank box. Writing correct numbers in the blank boxes based on a pattern.
Counting objects in a picture and identifying the number of each category. Matching pictures with the same pictures or dots.
Observing a picture, counting each thing, and writing the number in the blank box (up to 9).
Counting objects and representing the quantity with the number 7.
Counting objects and representing the quantity with the number 8.
Counting objects and representing the quantity with the number 9.
Matching the number of fingers shown with the corresponding numeral from 6 to 9.
Practicing writing the numerals from 6 to 9.
Counting objects in sets and identifying the set with a different quantity.
Drawing a specific number of objects based on a given quantity.
Counting objects and writing the corresponding numeral.
Practicing writing the numerals from 1 to 9 in ascending and descending order.
Counting objects and matching them to corresponding numbers, identifying missing numbers in a sequence.
Understanding the concepts of 'before', 'after', and 'between' in the context of a sequence of numbers using a train bogie example.
Comparing the number of objects to understand the concepts of 'more than', 'less than', and 'equal'.

chapters.chapter_title: Addition of  Numbers Total not Exceeding 9

topics.concept_summary: Pupils can say that if 1 is added to a number, we get the next number. They can count each bead in a chain and add them. They can write two numbers (upto 9) horizontally or vertically and add them. They can say how much is to be added to a number to get desired number. They can add things in two different groups and their total. They can add different numbers to get the same total. They understand that zero added to any number gives the same number.
Comparing two sets of objects to determine which has more, less, or equal items.
Counting pictures and identifying the set with more items, marking it with '9'.
Counting boxes, writing numbers, and reading them in ascending and descending order.
Identifying the next, middle, and previous numbers in a sequence.
Identifying the bigger and smaller of two given numbers.
Identifying the biggest of three numbers and the least of three numbers.
Extending a sequence of numbers from smallest to biggest.
Arranging numbers from smallest to biggest.
Arranging numbers from biggest to smallest.
Understanding the concept of zero as 'none' or 'nothing'.
Counting beads and representing the absence of beads with the number zero.
Counting rats and balloons and writing their number in the blank box.

topics.topic_name: Adding 1 to a Group of Beads
topics.concept_summary: Adding 1 to a group of beads and counting the total number of beads.
Adding one group of things to another group and counting the total.
Calculating the total of numbers by drawing dots and understanding vertical and horizontal addition.
Understanding that adding zero to any number results in the number itself.

chapters.chapter_title: Addition of Numbers, the Total not Exceeding 20

topics.concept_summary: Matching numbers with corresponding quantities of objects.
Adding two single-digit numbers and writing the sum.
Determining the missing number in an addition equation.
Adding pairs of numbers and identifying the pair with a different sum.
Understanding and performing subtraction of numbers up to 9 using pictures and examples.
Performing subtraction both horizontally and vertically.
Practicing subtraction problems.
Matching subtraction problems with the same answer.
Subtracting pairs of numbers and identifying the pair with a different difference.

topics.topic_name: Filling in Missing Numbers in Subtraction
topics.concept_summary: Filling in the missing number in a subtraction equation.
Understanding the concept of numbers from 10 to 20 using bundles of ten and loose objects.

topics.topic_name: Counting and Circling Groups of Ten
topics.concept_summary: Identifying and circling groups of ten objects.

topics.topic_name: Representing Numbers 10-14 with Bundles and Sticks
topics.concept_summary: Representing numbers from 10 to 14 using bundles of ten sticks and individual sticks.
Adding two numbers with a sum not exceeding 20, using bundles of sticks and loose sticks.
Subtracting a single-digit number from a two-digit number, using bundles of sticks and loose sticks.

topics.topic_name: Counting to 20 with Bundles and Sticks
topics.concept_summary: Counting sticks and bundles of sticks to represent numbers from 15 to 20. Understanding that adding 1 to 9 results in 10.
Understanding tens and ones using bundles and loose sticks to represent numbers 11 and 12. Reading and writing these numbers.
Understanding tens and ones using bundles and loose sticks to represent numbers 13, 14, 15 and 16. Reading and writing these numbers.
Understanding tens and ones using bundles and loose sticks to represent numbers 17, 18, 19 and 20. Recognizing 20 as two tens. Reading and writing these numbers.
Practice identifying and writing numbers from 10 to 20, including filling in missing numbers in a sequence.
Identifying the first, last, and middle numbers in a given sequence.
Comparing two numbers less than 20 and identifying the bigger number.

chapters.chapter_title: Number that come Before, Between and After

topics.concept_summary: Identifying bigger, smaller, biggest, and smallest numbers from a given set of numbers.
Identifying the object or group that is different from others in a row.
Adding two numbers where the total does not exceed 20.
Adding two numbers horizontally.

topics.topic_name: Adding Numbers Using Bundles and Loose Sticks
topics.concept_summary: Adding two numbers using bundles of sticks (representing tens) and loose sticks (representing ones).
Adding two numbers and writing the answer in a box.
Adding two numbers and coloring the part of a figure (turtle) that contains the answer.

topics.topic_name: Adding Pairs of Numbers and Identifying Different Answers
topics.concept_summary: Adding pairs of numbers in a row and identifying the different answer.
Subtracting a single-digit number from a two-digit number using bundles and loose sticks.
Subtracting numbers by looking at pictures and counting the remaining objects.
Subtracting two numbers.

topics.topic_name: Subtracting Pairs of Numbers and Identifying Different Answers
topics.concept_summary: Subtracting pairs of numbers and identifying the different answer.
Counting in tens and writing the numbers in boxes. Understanding the numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 and 100.

topics.topic_name: Counting Tens and Identifying Different Boxes
topics.concept_summary: Counting the tens in boxes and identifying the box which is different.
Counting the grids and writing the correct number.
Counting the beads in each chain and writing the correct number.
Matching each group of tens with the correct number.

topics.topic_name: Writing Correct Numbers in Blank Spaces
topics.concept_summary: Looking at the tens in each row and writing the correct number in the blank spaces.
Counting bundles of sticks and loose sticks, writing the number, and saying how many tens and ones are there in each number.
Pupils can identify different times of a complete day (morning, afternoon, daytime, evening, night) and say what people do at those times.
Pupils learn what they must do at what time.
Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 66 to 70. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.
Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 71 to 75. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.
Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 76 to 80. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.
Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 81 to 85. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.
Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 86 to 90. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.
Counting bundles of sticks (tens) and loose sticks (ones) to represent numbers from 91 to 95. Writing the numbers as sums of tens and ones.
